hints.nominative: Nominative masculine plural would be 'hi' — 'Horum' with -orum is genitive plural
hints.dative: Dative plural would be 'his' — 'Horum' is genitive plural
hints.accusative: Accusative masculine plural would be 'hos' — 'Horum' is genitive

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'omnes' — 'omnium' with -ium is genitive plural
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'omnes/omnia' — 'omnium' is genitive
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'omnibus' — 'omnium' is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'omnis' has genitive in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'omnis' has genitive in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'omnis' has genitive in -is

hints.positive genitive singular: Positive genitive singular would be 'fortis' — 'fortissimi' with -issim- is superlative
hints.comparative nominative plural: Comparative nominative plural would be 'fortiores' — 'fortissimi' with -issim- is superlative
hints.superlative genitive singular: While -i could be genitive singular, here 'fortissimi' agrees with 'Belgae' as nominative plural

hints.to stand: That would be 'stare' — 'sunt' comes from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go: That would be 'ire' — 'sunt' comes from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to know: That would be 'scire' — 'sunt' comes from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sint' — 'sunt' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuerunt' — 'sunt' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'erant' — 'sunt' is present indicative

hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand)
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the parts of 'dare' (to give)

hints.genitive: While 'Belgae' could be genitive singular, here it is the nominative plural subject of 'sunt'
hints.dative: Dative singular would also be 'Belgae', but here it is nominative plural — the subject
hints.vocative: Caesar is describing the Belgae, not addressing them — 'Belgae' is nominative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive plural in -orum — 'Belgarum' with -arum is 1st declension
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive plural in -um/-ium — 'Belgarum' is 1st declension
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive plural in -erum — 'Belgarum' is 1st declension

translation: Of all these, the Belgae are the bravest
